title: ext_type: wire-format@1
description: Extension block for binary wire formats and serialization framings — the byte-level layout of a unit on the wire (TCP/IPv4/UDP headers, DNS message, WebSocket frame, MQTT fixed header, protobuf field, ASN.1 BER/DER TLV, LEB128 varint, CBOR/MessagePack items). Captures the field-by-field structure plus an illustrative hex example. Hex strings are written in plain printable form (space- or unspaced hex digits), never as literal control bytes.

spec.description: The defining specification, normalized where possible (e.g. 'RFC793', 'RFC791', 'ITU-T X.690', 'protobuf encoding docs'). Single canonical reference; cross-references go in core see_also / attribution.

summary.description: Optional one-paragraph plain-text description of the format and, where relevant, the TLV (tag-length-value) pattern it embodies.

structure.description: Ordered field layout of one unit on the wire. Each row is one field with its size and meaning, top-to-bottom / first-to-last on the wire.

size.description: Field width as printed in the spec, e.g. '16 bits', '4 bits', '1-9 bytes', 'variable', '32 bits'.

meaning.description: What the field carries / how it is interpreted.

example_hex.description: Optional illustrative encoding of one concrete unit, as printable hex (space-separated or contiguous hex digits). No literal control bytes.

example_decoded.description: Optional human-readable decoding of `example_hex`, explaining what each chunk means.

see.description: Optional related entry ids within wire.phall.io (mirrors core see_also for renderer convenience).

notes.description: Optional format-level gotchas / clarifications. Free text, one per item.
